programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities 4
programme.desc: The host shows creatures that amaze scientists.It presents interesting facts and explains where myths about certain animals come from.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Once Upon A Time In Space
programme.desc: The creators of the four-part series take viewers on a journey through the history of the conquest of space.They give a voice to those who were involved in groundbreaking events.They show known stories from a new, very personal perspective.Astronauts, engineers and NASA employees, as well as specialists from the Russian space program, speak in front of the cameras.Their memories reveal a picture of great rivalry between superpowers, technological breakthroughs and emotions accompanying crossing new borders.A Great Leap for Mankind The first episode of this chronicle focuses on American astronauts who blazed unknown trails.The adversities they faced reflect the prejudices of the era.In 1978, black Ron McNair and Anna Fisher were selected by NASA to fly the newly unveiled Space Shuttle.For the first time in the history of the agency, candidates began to be accepted regardless of race or gender.Interviews and archival photos reveal the atmosphere of an era marked by fierce rivalry between the United States and the Soviet Union.In addition to moments of triumph, filmmakers recall tragedies such as the Challenger disaster.They show how the future of space flights is shaped in the context of history.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Our planet's landmasses are surrounded by shallow seas that are rarely more than 200 meters deep.They are located on continental shelves, stretching even hundreds of kilometers - until the bottom drops out and deeper, lightless waters begin.Together they constitute only eight percent of the world's ocean surface.However, most marine organisms live there.The shallow waters around the equator are perfect for baby whales because they are warm and calm.Few predators swim in them.Most of the tropical shallows have extremely poor waters, while the Great Barrier Reef is rich in marine organisms.These coral shelters are inhabited by countless species of fish, sea snakes and octopuses.The seabed of the shallow waters of Shark Bay in Western Australia is covered with underwater meadows.

programme.title: Life Below Zero 8
programme.desc: Despite climate change, the Hailstons do not give up their traditional lifestyle.Sue Aikens creates new tools, and Jessie Holmes and his dogs try to acclimatize to new areas.

programme.title: Life Below Zero 8
programme.desc: Despite climate change, the Hailstons do not give up their traditional lifestyle.Sue Aikens creates new tools, and Jessie Holmes and his dogs try to acclimatize to new areas.

programme.title: Treasures Decoded 5
programme.desc: The creators of the series conduct archaeological investigations into unsolved mysteries of antiquity.The emergence of new evidence gives researchers further opportunities to solve each of them.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Our planet's landmasses are surrounded by shallow seas that are rarely more than 200 meters deep.They are located on continental shelves, stretching even hundreds of kilometers - until the bottom drops out and deeper, lightless waters begin.Together they constitute only eight percent of the world's ocean surface.However, most marine organisms live there.The shallow waters around the equator are perfect for baby whales because they are warm and calm.Few predators swim in them.Most of the tropical shallows have extremely poor waters, while the Great Barrier Reef is rich in marine organisms.These coral shelters are inhabited by countless species of fish, sea snakes and octopuses.The seabed of the shallow waters of Shark Bay in Western Australia is covered with underwater meadows.

programme.title: Wonders of the Sun with Dara Ó Briain
Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Where the Wild Men Are with Ben Fogle
programme.desc: In the next season of the program, traveler Ben Fogle visits snowboarding legend Mike Basich, who abandoned the sport and started a new life in a self-built cabin in the Sierra Nevada mountains.He also goes to visit Bette, who has moved to the Australian outback and is struggling with snakes and floods.Rhona gave up her career in Hollywood and chose a lonely life in the bosom of nature in Uruguay.Ben helps her take care of the horses and discovers that thanks to her new life, the woman has felt peace and satisfaction for years.

programme.title: Once Upon A Time In Space
programme.desc: The creators of the four-part series take viewers on a journey through the history of the conquest of space.They give a voice to those who were involved in groundbreaking events.They show known stories from a new, very personal perspective.Astronauts, engineers and NASA employees, as well as specialists from the Russian space program, speak in front of the cameras.Their memories reveal a picture of great rivalry between superpowers, technological breakthroughs and emotions accompanying crossing new borders.A Great Leap for Mankind The first episode of this chronicle focuses on American astronauts who blazed unknown trails.The adversities they faced reflect the prejudices of the era.In 1978, black Ron McNair and Anna Fisher were selected by NASA to fly the newly unveiled Space Shuttle.For the first time in the history of the agency, candidates began to be accepted regardless of race or gender.Interviews and archival photos reveal the atmosphere of an era marked by fierce rivalry between the United States and the Soviet Union.In addition to moments of triumph, filmmakers recall tragedies such as the Challenger disaster.They show how the future of space flights is shaped in the context of history.

programme.title: Treasures Decoded 5
programme.desc: The creators of the series conduct archaeological investigations into unsolved mysteries of antiquity.The emergence of new evidence gives researchers further opportunities to solve each of them.

programme.title: Planet Earth 2
programme.desc: A fascinating natural spectacle.The creators of the program show the wealth of fauna and flora that inhabit the world.They visit all continents, where they observe commonly known and very rare species of animals and plants.Using the development of technology, they show life on Earth from a completely new perspective.They allow you to look at the world through the eyes of the creatures that inhabit it.Cameras placed very close record the normally elusive behavior of wild creatures.High-quality photos allow viewers to be in the very center of events - births, hunts or fights for leadership in the herd.All this is accompanied by commentary by David Attenborough, the famous naturalist, traveler and journalist.The series was divided into six parts.The first one reveals the richness of life on islands - from volcanic ones, through equatorial paradises, to Antarctica.The second episode focuses on mountains, the third - jungles, the fourth about deserts, and the fifth about steppes and savannas.The last one will present the life of animals in cities.

programme.title: Changing Planet
programme.desc: The aim of a special BBC research project is to document the climate changes that have occurred on Earth over seven years.The station's presenters visit the most endangered ecosystems.

programme.title: Where the Wild Men Are with Ben Fogle
programme.desc: In the next season of the program, traveler Ben Fogle visits snowboarding legend Mike Basich, who abandoned the sport and started a new life in a self-built cabin in the Sierra Nevada mountains.He also goes to visit Bette, who has moved to the Australian outback and is struggling with snakes and floods.Rhona gave up her career in Hollywood and chose a lonely life in the bosom of nature in Uruguay.Ben helps her take care of the horses and discovers that thanks to her new life, the woman has felt peace and satisfaction for years.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Australia's Health Revolution with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Dr. Michael Mosley and physiologist Ray Kelly challenge people suffering from type 2 diabetes with an eating plan that can improve the symptoms of their disease in just a few days.Viewers will find out whether a radical diet based on numerous scientific studies can be the key to controlling the diabetes epidemic in Australia.For the good of humanity, Mosley also decides to experience the effects of a certain diet for himself.The doctor follows a menu consisting of sweet carbohydrates and saturated fats.He wants to make viewers aware of the impact of processed food on human health.

programme.title: Surgeons: A Matter Of Life Or Death
programme.desc: More stories of life-saving operations in one of the largest hospitals in Great Britain.The series shows up close the activities and work of surgeons with extraordinary skills.

programme.title: Treasures Decoded 5
programme.desc: The creators of the series conduct archaeological investigations into unsolved mysteries of antiquity.The emergence of new evidence gives researchers further opportunities to solve each of them.

programme.title: Life Below Zero 8
programme.desc: Despite climate change, the Hailstons do not give up their traditional lifestyle.Sue Aikens creates new tools, and Jessie Holmes and his dogs try to acclimatize to new areas.

programme.title: Life Below Zero 8
programme.desc: Despite climate change, the Hailstons do not give up their traditional lifestyle.Sue Aikens creates new tools, and Jessie Holmes and his dogs try to acclimatize to new areas.

programme.title: Treasures Decoded 5
programme.desc: The creators of the series conduct archaeological investigations into unsolved mysteries of antiquity.The emergence of new evidence gives researchers further opportunities to solve each of them.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Our planet's landmasses are surrounded by shallow seas that are rarely more than 200 meters deep.They are located on continental shelves, stretching even hundreds of kilometers - until the bottom drops out and deeper, lightless waters begin.Together they constitute only eight percent of the world's ocean surface.However, most marine organisms live there.The shallow waters around the equator are perfect for baby whales because they are warm and calm.Few predators swim in them.Most of the tropical shallows have extremely poor waters, while the Great Barrier Reef is rich in marine organisms.These coral shelters are inhabited by countless species of fish, sea snakes and octopuses.The seabed of the shallow waters of Shark Bay in Western Australia is covered with underwater meadows.

programme.title: Where the Wild Men Are with Ben Fogle
programme.desc: In the next season of the program, traveler Ben Fogle visits snowboarding legend Mike Basich, who abandoned the sport and started a new life in a self-built cabin in the Sierra Nevada mountains.He also goes to visit Bette, who has moved to the Australian outback and is struggling with snakes and floods.Rhona gave up her career in Hollywood and chose a lonely life in the bosom of nature in Uruguay.Ben helps her take care of the horses and discovers that thanks to her new life, the woman has felt peace and satisfaction for years.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities 4
programme.desc: The host shows creatures that amaze scientists.It presents interesting facts and explains where myths about certain animals come from.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Islands: Nature's Wild Laboratories
programme.desc: Baffin is the largest island in the Canadian Arctic.In summer, when the ice recedes, animals must adapt to new conditions.Polar bears hunt seals off the shore and climb cliffs in search of nesting birds.The camera also follows arctic foxes raising their cubs and herds of white belugas.There is also the first-ever footage of a narwhal with two horns.This extraordinary material shows how strongly climate change affects this harsh yet vibrant ecosystem.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Trees are among the most wonderful creations of nature.The most extensive forests on our planet are deciduous and coniferous forests, growing in the zone of changing seasons.They managed to control such large areas thanks to their ability to survive the changes of seasons.The further from the North Pole, the more dominant the role of trees in the landscape becomes.Most of them are in the taiga, although development opportunities there are extremely difficult.In this respect, conditions in Californian forests are more favorable, where trees reach enormous sizes.In the nearby mountains you can admire the oldest organisms on our planet - ancient pines.Some have been growing there for up to five thousand years.

programme.title: Life Below Zero 8
programme.desc: Despite climate change, the Hailstons do not give up their traditional lifestyle.Sue Aikens creates new tools, and Jessie Holmes and his dogs try to acclimatize to new areas.

programme.title: Life Below Zero 8
programme.desc: Despite climate change, the Hailstons do not give up their traditional lifestyle.Sue Aikens creates new tools, and Jessie Holmes and his dogs try to acclimatize to new areas.

programme.title: Treasures Decoded 5
programme.desc: The creators of the series conduct archaeological investigations into unsolved mysteries of antiquity.The emergence of new evidence gives researchers further opportunities to solve each of them.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Trees are among the most wonderful creations of nature.The most extensive forests on our planet are deciduous and coniferous forests, growing in the zone of changing seasons.They managed to control such large areas thanks to their ability to survive the changes of seasons.The further from the North Pole, the more dominant the role of trees in the landscape becomes.Most of them are in the taiga, although development opportunities there are extremely difficult.In this respect, conditions in Californian forests are more favorable, where trees reach enormous sizes.In the nearby mountains you can admire the oldest organisms on our planet - ancient pines.Some have been growing there for up to five thousand years.

programme.title: James Webb: The $10 Billion Space Telescope
programme.desc: The James Webb Space Telescope is the largest and most advanced in the world.It allows you to explore and photograph planets outside the solar system, as well as trace the structure of the universe.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Where the Wild Men Are with Ben Fogle
programme.desc: In the next season of the program, traveler Ben Fogle visits snowboarding legend Mike Basich, who abandoned the sport and started a new life in a self-built cabin in the Sierra Nevada mountains.He also goes to visit Bette, who has moved to the Australian outback and is struggling with snakes and floods.Rhona gave up her career in Hollywood and chose a lonely life in the bosom of nature in Uruguay.Ben helps her take care of the horses and discovers that thanks to her new life, the woman has felt peace and satisfaction for years.

programme.title: Islands: Nature's Wild Laboratories
programme.desc: Baffin is the largest island in the Canadian Arctic.In summer, when the ice recedes, animals must adapt to new conditions.Polar bears hunt seals off the shore and climb cliffs in search of nesting birds.The camera also follows arctic foxes raising their cubs and herds of white belugas.There is also the first-ever footage of a narwhal with two horns.This extraordinary material shows how strongly climate change affects this harsh yet vibrant ecosystem.

programme.title: Treasures Decoded 5
programme.desc: The creators of the series conduct archaeological investigations into unsolved mysteries of antiquity.The emergence of new evidence gives researchers further opportunities to solve each of them.

programme.title: Planet Earth 2
programme.desc: A fascinating natural spectacle.The creators of the program show the wealth of fauna and flora that inhabit the world.They visit all continents, where they observe commonly known and very rare species of animals and plants.Using the development of technology, they show life on Earth from a completely new perspective.They allow you to look at the world through the eyes of the creatures that inhabit it.Cameras placed very close record the normally elusive behavior of wild creatures.High-quality photos allow viewers to be in the very center of events - births, hunts or fights for leadership in the herd.All this is accompanied by commentary by David Attenborough, the famous naturalist, traveler and journalist.The series was divided into six parts.The first one reveals the richness of life on islands - from volcanic ones, through equatorial paradises, to Antarctica.The second episode focuses on mountains, the third - jungles, the fourth about deserts, and the fifth about steppes and savannas.The last one will present the life of animals in cities.

programme.title: Sue Perkins Into Alaska
programme.desc: Actress Sue Perkins sets off on a journey through Alaska, the largest American state, famous for its harsh climate and wildlife.The expedition begins in the most populated city, Anchorage.

programme.title: Where the Wild Men Are with Ben Fogle
programme.desc: In the next season of the program, traveler Ben Fogle visits snowboarding legend Mike Basich, who abandoned the sport and started a new life in a self-built cabin in the Sierra Nevada mountains.He also goes to visit Bette, who has moved to the Australian outback and is struggling with snakes and floods.Rhona gave up her career in Hollywood and chose a lonely life in the bosom of nature in Uruguay.Ben helps her take care of the horses and discovers that thanks to her new life, the woman has felt peace and satisfaction for years.
